text: The surgeon begins by moving to the medial area to dissect lymph nodes and soft tissues along the inferior mesenteric artery. The dissection follows the plane between the fascia and mesocolon, expanding the plane to free the sigmoid colon. Care is taken to identify and preserve the left ureter during this process.

The surgeon skeletonizes the distal rectum and closes the rectal lumen at the distal margin with ligation. The distal rectum is washed from the anus, and the proximal margins of the resection are confirmed. The proximal colon lumen is closed by ligation, and after repeated washing, the distal rectal lumen is clean. The bowel is then transected using a harmonic scalpel.

The surgeon separates the bowel wall using a sterilized film and places the stapler anvil into the abdominal cavity through the rectal lumen from the anus. The specimen is amputated from the proximal colon and extracted through the anus. The distal lumen is closed using an Ethicon EndoGIA stapler, and the area is washed multiple times. The stapler anvil is then placed into the proximal lumen to complete the anastomosis.

The surgeon performs bubble testing to confirm there is no air leakage from the anastomosis site. The mesocolon hiatus is closed surgically, followed by closure of the chakra incisions. The procedure concludes with no tubes left in the patient post-surgery.
